title: haloragis heterophylla
intro: Haloragis heterophylla, also known as the floating fern or water dodonea, is a plant species native to Australia and New Zealand. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat skin conditions and wounds due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant has been shown to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, fighting infections, and promoting wound healing. Haloragis heterophylla can be prepared as a tea or infusion, or as a topical application, to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
